title: Overdose diagnostic and treatment device and method
abstract: Devices and methods for diagnosing and administering drug overdose countermeasure. In some embodiments, a device includes a analyzer with a decision support application electronically connected to a drug of abuse detection device and a non-invasive blood gas device that support specific identification of drugs of abuse within a patient's system. In some embodiments, a method includes inputting patient demographics into the analyzer, inputting a patient's saliva, blood, and/or urine sample into the drug of abuse detection device, operatively affixing a non- invasive blood gas device to a patient, and administering a proper countermeasure dosage analyzer to the patient experiencing drug overdose.
claims:
Claims What is claimed is: 1. A system for detection and countermeasure of drug overdose comprising: an analyzer; and a drug detection apparatus adapted to receive a sample from a person containing at least one drug, determine an identity of the at least one drug, determine a concentration of the at least one drug in the sample, and transmit said identity and concentration to the analyzer; wherein the analyzer is adapted to receive said identity and concentration from the drug detection apparatus, and to receive information about the person including age, ethnicity, sex, weight, and/or race, and is further adapted to determine, based on said identity, concentration, and/or information, an overdose countermeasure for at least partially counteracting an overdose condition of the person. 2. A system as defined in claim 1, further including a blood gas apparatus adapted to measure at least one vital sign of the person including an amount of at least one blood gas in the personâs blood and transmit said at least one vital sign to the analyzer, wherein the analyzer is adapted to receive the at least one vital sign from the blood gas apparatus, and to determine, based on said identity, concentration, at least one vital sign and/or information, an overdose countermeasure for at least partially counteracting an overdose condition of the person. 3. A system as defined in claim 2, wherein the blood gas apparatus is further adapted to measure a heart rate and/or respiration rate of the person. 4. A system as defined in claim 2 or 3, wherein the analyzer is further adapted to determine whether the person has an overdose condition based on the at least one vital sign. 5. A system as defined in claim 4, wherein the analyzer includes or is operatively connected or connectable to a first database comprising information including normal ranges of the at least one vital sign and/or vital sign changes associated with the overdose condition, and the analyzer is adapted to determine whether the person has an overdose condition based on the first database information. 6. A system as defined in any of claims 2 to 5, wherein the analyzer is adapted to determine whether the countermeasure at least partially reverses the overdose condition based on 

at least one vital sign of the person measured by the blood gas apparatus after administration of the countermeasure to the person. 7. A system as defined in claim 6, wherein the analyzer includes or is operatively connected or connectable to a first database comprising information including normal ranges of the at least one vital sign and/or vital sign changes associated with the overdose condition, and the analyzer is adapted to determine whether the countermeasure at least partially reverses the overdose condition based on the first database information. 8. A system as defined in any of the preceding claims, wherein the countermeasure includes at least one dose of a countermeasure drug. 9. A system as defined in claim 7, wherein the countermeasure includes multiple doses of a countermeasure drug, and the analyzer is further adapted to determine a time period between administration of a first of said multiple doses and a second of said multiple doses. 10. A system as defined in claim 8 or 9, wherein the at least one drug includes an opioid and the countermeasure drug includes naloxone. 11. A system as defined in any of claims 8 to 10, wherein the system further includes at least one dose of the countermeasure drug. 12. A system as defined in any of the preceding claims, wherein the drug detection apparatus includes a capillary electrophoretic device. 13. A system as defined in any of the preceding claims, further including at least one sample collection tool adapted to collect a sample containing at least one drug from the person. 14. A system as defined in claim 13, wherein the at least one sample collection tool is adapted to collect a sample of saliva, blood and/or urine from the person. 15. A system as defined in any of the preceding claims, wherein the drug detection apparatus includes a sample receiving port adapted to receive the sample. 16. A system as defined in any of claims 2 to 7, wherein the blood gas apparatus is adapted to non-invasively measure the at least one vital sign. 17. A system as defined in any of claims 2 to 7, wherein the blood gas apparatus is adapted to measure blood oxygen and/or blood carbon dioxide levels of a person. 18. A system as defined in any of the preceding claims, wherein the analyzer includes or is operatively connected or connectable to a second database comprising information including pharmacokinetic data of the at least one drug. 19. A system as defined in claim 18, wherein the second database further comprises information regarding effect of age, ethnicity, sex, weight, and/or race on metabolism of the at least one drug. 20. A system as defined in claim 18 or 19, wherein the countermeasure includes a countermeasure drug, and the second database further comprises information including pharmacokinetic data of the countermeasure drug. 21. A system as defined in any of claims 18-20, wherein the second database further comprises effect of age, ethnicity, sex, weight, and/or race on metabolism of the countermeasure drug and/or effect of formulation and administration mechanism of the countermeasure drug. 22. A system as defined in any of claims 18 to 21, wherein the analyzer is adapted to determine the countermeasure based on said second database information. 23. A system as defined in any of the preceding claims, wherein the analyzer is further adapted to generate a communication of the countermeasure for a user of the system. 24. A system as defined in claim 23, wherein the communication is visual and/or audible. 25. A system as defined in claim 24, wherein the communication is visual, and the analyzer includes or is operatively connected or connectable to a graphical user interface adapted to display the communication. 26. A system as defined in any of the preceding claims, wherein the analyzer includes or is operatively connected or connectable to at least one input device adapted to receive the information about the person from a user of the system. 27. A system as defined in claim 26, wherein the at least one input device includes a touchscreen, a keyboard, a microphone and/or a camera. 28. A system as defined in claim 2, wherein the analyzer, drug detection apparatus and blood gas apparatus are located within a housing. 29. A system as defined in in claim 28, wherein the housing is transformable between a closed condition wherein the analyzer, drug detection apparatus and blood gas apparatus are not accessible to a user, and an open condition wherein the analyzer, drug detection apparatus and blood gas apparatus are accessible to a user. 30. A system for detection and countermeasure of drug overdose comprising: first means; and second means for receiving a sample from a person containing at least one drug, for determining an identity of the at least one drug, for determining a concentration of the at least one drug in the sample, and for transmitting said identity and concentration to the first means; wherein the first means is for receiving said identity and concentration from the second means, for receiving information about the person including age, ethnicity, sex, weight, and/or race, and for determining, based on said identity, concentration, and/or information, an overdose countermeasure for at least partially counteracting an overdose condition of the person. 31. A system as defined in claim 30, further including third means for measuring at least one vital sign of the person including an amount of at least one blood gas in the personâs blood and for transmitting said at least one vital sign to the first means, wherein the first means is further for receiving the at least one vital sign from the third means, and for determining, based on said identity, concentration, at least one vital sign and/or information, an overdose countermeasure for at least partially counteracting an overdose condition of the person. 32. A system as defined in claim 31, wherein the analyzer is further for determining whether the person has an overdose condition based on the at least one vital sign and/or 

determining whether the countermeasure at least partially reverses the overdose condition based on at least one vital sign of the person measured by the third means after administration of the countermeasure to the person. 33. A system as defined in any of claims 30 to 32, further including fourth means for collecting a sample containing at least one drug from the person. 34. A system as defined in claim 33, wherein the fourth means is for collecting a sample of saliva, blood and/or urine from the person. 35. A system as defined in claim 33 or 34, wherein the fourth means includes at least one sample collection tool. 36. A system as defined in any of claims 30 to 35, further including fifth means for counteracting an overdose condition of the patient. 37. A system as defined in claim 36, wherein the fifth means includes a countermeasure drug. 38. A system as defined in any of claims 30 to 37, further including sixth means for user input of the information about the person. 39. A system as defined in claim 38, wherein the sixth means includes at least one input device. 40. A system as defined in any of claims 31 to 39, further including seventh means for housing the first, second and third means. 41. A system as defined in claim 40, wherein the seventh means includes a housing. 42. A system as defined in any of claims 31 to 41, wherein the first means includes an analyzer, the second means includes a drug detection apparatus, and the third means includes a blood gas apparatus. 43. A method for treating a drug overdose comprising: (a) collecting a sample from a person containing at least one drug; 

 (b) inputting the sample into a drug detection apparatus adapted to receive the sample, determine an identity of the at least one drug in the sample, and determine a concentration of the at least one drug in the sample; (c) inputting into an analyzer information about the person including age, ethnicity, sex, weight, and/or race, wherein the analyzer is operatively connected to the drug detection apparatus and adapted to receive said identity and concentration therefrom, and determine, based on said identity, concentration, and/or information, an overdose countermeasure for at least partially counteracting an overdose condition of the person; (d) perceiving at least one communication from the analyzer specifying a countermeasure for the overdose condition; and (e) administering the countermeasure to the person. 44. A method as defined in claim 43, further including: (f) operatively connecting a blood gas apparatus to the person, wherein the blood gas apparatus is adapted to measure at least one vital sign of the person including an amount of at least one blood gas in the personâs blood; wherein the analyzer is operatively connected to the blood gas apparatus and adapted to receive the at least one vital sign therefrom and to determine, based on said identity, concentration, at least one vital sign and/or information, an overdose countermeasure for at least partially counteracting an overdose condition of the person. 45. A method as defined in claim 43 or 44, wherein step (a) includes collecting saliva, blood and/or urine from the person. 46. A method as defined in any of claims 43 to 45, wherein the countermeasure includes a countermeasure drug and step (e) includes administering the countermeasure drug to the person in a dose specified by the at least one communication. 47. A method as defined in claim 46, wherein the at least one drug includes an opioid and the countermeasure drug includes naloxone. 48. A method as defined in claim 46 or 47, wherein the countermeasure includes multiple doses of the countermeasure drug, and step (e) includes administering to the person a 

first of said multiple doses at a first time and a second of said multiple doses at a second time later than the first time. 49. A method as defined in claim 48, wherein the at least one communication specifies the second time. 50. A method as defined in claim 48 or 49, further including inputting into the analyzer an input substantially representing said first time. 51. A method as defined in claim 44, wherein the at least one vital sign includes heart rate and/or respiration rate of the person. 52. A method as defined in claim 44 or 51, wherein the blood gas apparatus is a non- invasive blood gas apparatus. 53. A method as defined in any of claims 43 to 52, including administering the countermeasure until perceiving a further communication from the analyzer specifying to cease administering the countermeasure. 54. A method for countermeasure of drug overdose comprising: (a) receiving an identity and a concentration of at least one drug present in a sample from a person; (b) receiving information about the person including age, ethnicity, sex, weight, and/or race; and (c) determining an overdose countermeasure for at least partially counteracting an overdose condition of the person based on said identity, concentration, and/or information. 55. A method as defined in claim 54, further including: (d) receiving at least one vital sign of the person including an amount of at least one blood gas in the personâs blood; wherein step (c) comprises determining an overdose countermeasure for at least partially counteracting an overdose condition of the person based on said identity, concentration, at least one vital sign and/or information. 56. A method as defined in claim 54 or 55, further including generating a communication perceivable by a user configured to communicate the countermeasure to the user. 57. A method as defined in any of claims 54 to 56, wherein step (a) includes receiving said identity and concentration from a drug detection apparatus. 58. A method as defined in claim 55, wherein step (d) includes receiving said at least one vital sign from a blood gas apparatus. 59. A method as defined in claim 55 or 58, wherein the at least one vital sign includes a heart rate and/or respiration rate of the person and step (d) includes receiving said heart rate and/or respiration rate of the person. 60. A method as defined in any of claims 54 to 59, wherein step (b) includes receiving said information by input from a user via an input device. 61. A method as defined in claim 55, further including determining whether the person has an overdose condition based on the at least one vital sign. 62. A method as defined in claim 61, wherein the step of determining whether the person has an overdose condition includes determining whether the person has an overdose condition based on information including normal ranges of the at least one vital sign and/or vital sign changes associated with an overdose condition. 63. A method as defined in any of claims 54 to 62, further including, after administration of the countermeasure to the person, receiving at least one vital sign of the person and determining whether the countermeasure at least partially reverses the overdose condition based thereon. 64. A method as defined in claims 63, wherein the step of determining whether the countermeasure at least partially reverses the overdose condition includes determining whether the countermeasure at least partially reverses the overdose condition based on information including normal ranges of the at least one vital sign and/or vital sign changes associated with the overdose condition. 65. A method as defined in any of claims 54 to 64, wherein the countermeasure includes at least one dose of a countermeasure drug. 66. A method as defined in claim 65, wherein the countermeasure includes multiple doses of a countermeasure drug, and the method further comprises determining a time period between administration of a first of said multiple doses and a second of said multiple doses. 67. A method as defined in claim 66, wherein the method further comprises generating a communication perceivable by the user configured to communicate the time period to the user. 68. A method as defined in any of claims 65 to 67, wherein the at least one drug includes an opioid and the countermeasure drug includes naloxone. 69. A method as defined in any of claims 54 to 68, wherein the sample includes saliva, blood and/or urine from the person. 70. A method as defined in any of claims 54 to 69, wherein step (c) includes determining the countermeasure based on information including pharmacokinetic data of the at least one drug. 71. A method as defined in claim 70, wherein step (c) includes determining the countermeasure based on information regarding effect of age, ethnicity, sex, weight, and/or race on metabolism of the at least one drug. 72. A method as defined in claim 54, wherein the countermeasure includes a countermeasure drug, and step (c) includes determining the countermeasure based on information including pharmacokinetic data of the countermeasure drug. 73. A method as defined in claim 72, wherein step (c) includes determining the countermeasure based on information including age, ethnicity, sex, weight, and/or race on metabolism of the countermeasure drug and/or effect of formulation and administration mechanism of the countermeasure drug. 74. A non-transitory computer-readable medium having computer-readable instructions stored thereon that, when executed by a computer system, cause the computer system to perform the steps of: (a) receiving an identity and a concentration of at least one drug present in a sample from a person; (b) receiving information about the person including age, ethnicity, sex, weight, and/or race; and (c) determining an overdose countermeasure for at least partially counteracting an overdose condition of the person based on said identity, concentration, and/or information. 75. A computer-readable medium as defined in claim 74, wherein the computer- readable instructions, when executed by a computer system, further cause the computer system to perform the step of (d) receiving at least one vital sign of the person including an amount of at least one blood gas in the personâs blood; wherein step (c) comprises determining an overdose countermeasure for at least partially counteracting an overdose condition of the person based on said identity, concentration, at least one vital sign and/or information. 76. A computer-readable medium as defined in claim 74 or 75, wherein the computer- readable instructions, when executed by a computer system, further cause the computer system to perform the step of generating a communication perceivable by a user configured to communicate the countermeasure to the user. 77. A computer-readable medium as defined in any of claims 74 to 76, wherein step (a) includes receiving said identity and concentration from a drug detection apparatus. 78. A computer-readable medium as defined in claim 75, wherein step (d) includes receiving said at least one vital sign from a blood gas apparatus. 79. A computer-readable medium as defined in claim 75 or 78, wherein the at least one vital sign includes a heart rate and/or respiration rate of the person and step (d) includes receiving said heart rate and/or respiration rate of the person. 80. A computer-readable medium as defined in any of claims 74 to 79, wherein step (b) includes receiving said information by input from a user via an input device. 81. A computer-readable medium as defined in any of claim 75, wherein the computer-readable instructions, when executed by a computer system, further cause the computer system to perform the step of determining whether the person has an overdose condition based on the at least one vital sign. 82. A computer-readable medium as defined in claim 75, wherein the step of determining whether the person has an overdose condition includes determining whether the person has an overdose condition based on information including normal ranges of the at least one vital sign and/or vital sign changes associated with an overdose condition. 83. A computer-readable medium as defined in any of claims 74 to 82, wherein the computer-readable instructions, when executed by a computer system, further cause the computer system to perform the steps of, after administration of the countermeasure to the person, determining whether the countermeasure at least partially reverses the overdose condition based on at least one vital sign of the person. 84. A computer-readable medium as defined in any of claims 74 to 83, wherein the countermeasure includes at least one dose of a countermeasure drug. 85. A computer-readable medium as defined in claim 84, wherein the countermeasure includes multiple doses of a countermeasure drug, and the computer-readable instructions, when executed by a computer system, further cause the computer system to perform the step of determining a time period between administration of a first of said multiple doses and a second of said multiple doses. 86. A computer-readable medium as defined in claim 85, wherein the computer- readable instructions, when executed by a computer system, further cause the computer system to perform the step of generating a communication perceivable by the user configured to communicate the time period to the user. 87. A computer-readable medium as defined in any of claims 74 to 86, wherein the at least one drug includes an opioid and the countermeasure drug includes naloxone. 88. A computer-readable medium as defined in any of claims 74 to 87, wherein the sample includes saliva, blood and/or urine from the person. 89. A computer-readable medium as defined in any of claims 74 to 88, wherein step (c) includes determining the countermeasure based on information including pharmacokinetic data of the at least one drug. 90. A computer-readable medium as defined in claim 89, wherein step (c) includes determining the countermeasure based on information based on information regarding effect of age, ethnicity, sex, weight, and/or race on metabolism of the at least one drug. 91. A computer-readable medium as defined in claim 89 or 90, wherein the countermeasure includes a countermeasure drug, and step (c) includes determining the countermeasure based on information including pharmacokinetic data of the countermeasure drug. 92. A computer-readable medium as defined in claim 91, wherein step (c) includes determining the countermeasure based on information including age, ethnicity, sex, weight, and/or race on metabolism of the countermeasure drug and/or effect of formulation and administration mechanism of the countermeasure drug.
